Report: Biopharma Job Market Report Q2 2026
snippet: Gain the latest insights into the biopharma hiring landscape with BioSpace's Biopharma Job Market Report Q2 2026. Explore the trends shaping talent acquisition, from rising job postings and sustained R&D hiring to the market activity signaling stronger hiring demand ahead.

title: EMD Serono may trim R&D workforce by up to 70
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: BIO 2026: As ADCs evolve, experts say ‘wellness’ needs to be factored into patient survival
snippet: As antibody-drug conjugates advance and move into earlier lines of treatment, drug developers have to build gentler therapies that don’t just extend survival but improve it.

title: AB Science ends 3 studies in multiple sclerosis, other immune conditions
snippet: The trials, which were testing the tyrosine kinase inhibitor masitinib, had previously been paused. AB Science’s decision not to resume them was a matter of prioritization, not safety, the biotech said.

title: FDA posts new rejection letters days after confirming pause
snippet: The FDA last week confirmed that it would suspend the release of complete response letters pending the resolution of a citizen petition from an unnamed pharma company. Nevertheless, 14 new CRLs are now available on the agency’s portal.

title: The FDA needs a patient-first vision—and adcomms with patients at the table
snippet: FDA’s rare disease decisions are strongest when the patient community has a voice in advisory committee decisions.

title: From sequence to scale: Gene editing’s new era in biologics manufacturing
snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you’ll hear from Jack Crawford, CEO of Demeetra, and Magnus Gustavsson, chief commercial officer at NorthX Biologics. We unpack the evolution of cell line development — CHO cells, targeted integration, transposases and the collaboration models speeding biologics from sequence to GMP.

title: AAIC 2026: Tau-targeted Alzheimer’s treatments heat up while amyloid therapies persist
snippet: The lineup at the Alzheimer’s Association International Conference will provide critical insight into where the industry is headed with regard to targets being explored to vanquish the elusive neurodegenerative disease.

title: GSK and Hansoh’s ADC clinches late-stage win, teeing up regulatory submission in China
snippet: GSK and Hansoh Pharmaceutical’s antibody-drug conjugate success validates their partnership, one of the many deals in which Big Pharma has tapped a China company for promising cancer candidates.

title: AstraZeneca, Ionis’ Wainua woes send waves across ATTR-CM space
snippet: The failure of AstraZeneca and Ionis’ Wainua in a late-stage study of ATTR-CM casts doubt on Alnylam’s next-generation candidate but is good news for others in the space, including BridgeBio and Intellia Therapeutics.

title: Roche scraps 2 Huntington’s studies, handing Ionis more bad news
snippet: Roche’s decision to discontinue the Ionis-partnered trials came soon after the biotech sustained a late-stage failure in ATTR-CM.

title: Amgen continues to defend Tavneos as global regulatory pressure mounts
snippet: Regulators on both sides of the Atlantic are pushing for the withdrawal of the rare disease treatment that accounted for just 1% of Amgen’s 2025 revenue. Nevertheless, Amgen continues to defend the medicine, which was acquired in the $3.7 billion buyout of ChemoCentryx.

title: Biopharma R&D needs ‘structural redesign’ to maximize AI impact: report
snippet: Biopharma companies won’t fully capture the benefits of AI unless they reorganize their R&D units, according to McKinsey.

title: Cancer, rare disease dominate FDA approvals in H1 2026 as AstraZeneca, J&J clean up
snippet: The FDA greenlit 26 novel therapies in the first half of 2026, including four for cancer and six for orphan indications. Meanwhile, AstraZeneca and Johnson and Johnson took home a combined 11 of the agency’s 79 total approvals, including supplemental nods.

title: Psychedelics surge again as Compass, Definium provide more clinical validation
snippet: Psychedelics are gaining momentum in depression, with one treating physician predicting that the drug class could “wipe out the SSRIs” if safety and durability hold up.

title: Saol boards FDA’s resubmission boat, armed with new survival data
snippet: Saol Therapeutics is the latest biotech to resubmit for approval of a drug rejected under former FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, following REGENXBIO and Replimune.

title: AstraZeneca, Ionis slip as ATTR-CM drug fails Phase 3
snippet: The late-stage miss is “surprising,” Stifel analysts said, given that Wainua’s mechanism of silencing transthyretin protein expression has previously proven effective.

title: GSK abandons Alector after back-to-back neuro stumbles
snippet: GSK and Alector first partnered in 2021 to advance two antibodies for neurodegenerative diseases. Both assets have since failed to show significant clinical benefit.

title: FDA freezes CRL trove as ‘radical transparency’ push hits speedbump
snippet: An unnamed pharma filed a citizen petition in April seeking reforms to the way the FDA publicly releases rejection letters, alleging that the policy “contravenes decades of agency practice.”

title: Tanabe pens deal to sell plants to Towa in post-buyout pivot
snippet: After being bought by Bain for $3.3 billion, Tanabe has reached a deal to sell its manufacturing unit and 17 products.

title: Biotech’s China problem: An issue of national security or economic value for the US?
snippet: Congressional letters sent to the CEOs of Eli Lilly, Pfizer, Merck, BMS and AbbVie this week voicing concerns about the pharmas' clinical trials in China highlight an ongoing discrepancy in how government and industry think about the rise of the Asian country’s biotech industry.

title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

title: Hepatitis B core-related antigen as a multifaceted biomarker in chronic hepatitis B: implications for immune activity and hepatocellular carcinoma prediction.
publicationName: The Korean journal of internal medicine
journalName: The Korean journal of internal medicine
snippet: Hepatitis B core-related antigen (HBcrAg) reflects both serum hepatitis B virus (HBV) DNA and intrahepatic covalently closed circular DNA activity, offering potential advantages over conventional biomarkers in monitoring chronic hepatitis B (CHB). This study evaluated the clinical utility of HBcrAg across immune phases and its prognostic value for hepatocel...

title: Shuyu Wan Potentiates PD-1 Inhibitor Efficacy in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer: Integrated Bioinformatics and Experimental Evidence for Gut Microbiota-Tumor Immune Crosstalk.
snippet: Therapeutic heterogeneity limits the efficacy of immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). Shuyu Wan (SYW), a classic TCM formula, has shown potential in modulating gut microbiota (GM) and enhancing immunotherapy, yet its synergistic mechanism with PD-1 inhibitors remains unclear. SYW components were identified by UPLC-MS. N...

title: MRI Monitored Mn-THPPmPEG12-Guided Phototherapy Elicits Abscopal Immunity and Synergizes with PD-1 Blockade in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer in Mice Model.
snippet: The study was to develop a clinical-translational theranostic photosensitizer, Mn-THPPmPEG12, for multiparametric MRI-monitored photodynamic therapy (PDT) of triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), and to evaluate its potential to elicit abscopal immunity and synergize with PD-1 blockade. title: Preoperative quality of life in patients undergoing liver surgery.
snippet: Surgical resection remains a cornerstone in the treatment of various hepatobiliary diseases. However, preoperative quality of life (QoL) in patients undergoing liver resection remains insufficiently studied. This study aimed to evaluate preoperative QoL and identify factors associated with impaired QoL in patients scheduled for liver surgery. This single-ce...

title: PIN1 inhibits ferroptosis in gastric cancer cells by regulating the CPEB1‑GPX4 pathway.
snippet: Ferroptosis, an iron‑dependent form of programmed cell death, is a promising target for cancer therapy. Peptidyl‑prolyl cis/trans isomerase 1 (PIN1), a member of the peptidyl‑prolyl cis/trans isomerase family, is often overexpressed in cancer and contributes to tumor cell proliferation, survival and metastasis. title: Salivary Methotrexate as a Predictor of Oral Mucositis in Hematological Cancer Patients: A Phase II Randomized, Double-Blind Clinical Trial.
snippet: To evaluate the efficacy of prophylactic photobiomodulation therapy (PBMT) in preventing oral mucositis (OM) and to assess serum and salivary methotrexate (MTX) levels as predictors of OM in adults receiving high-dose methotrexate (HD-MTX). title: Translating whole-genome doubling into precision medicine in cancer.
snippet: Whole-genome doubling (WGD) occurs in approximately 30-40% of human cancers, representing a transformative evolutionary event that fundamentally reshapes tumor biology. WGD triggers chromosomal instability (CIN) and somatic copy-number alterations (SCNAs), which accelerate tumor evolution and underlie aggressive clinical behavior. Paradoxically, the same ce...

title: Comparison of Relapse Risk Prediction Models During Active Surveillance in Stage I Seminoma: A Retrospective Multicenter Cohort Study.
snippet: To improve the prediction of relapse for clinical stage I seminoma, we aimed to validate the Boormans model (incorporating tumor size, rete testis invasion, and lymphovascular invasion) against the conventional classification (tumor size > 4 cm/rete testis invasion). title: Atherosclerotic Cardiovascular Disease and Cancer.
snippet: Atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) and cancer are increasingly recognized as interconnected diseases linked by shared immune mechanisms rather than merely overlapping risk factors. title: Non-canonical function of transferrin receptor-1 promotes breast cancer metastasis by activating HCK‒STAT3‒MMP9 signalling.
snippet: Transferrin receptor 1 (TfR-1), a key mediator of cellular iron uptake, is significantly upregulated across a broad spectrum of malignancies, and its overexpression highly correlated with poor clinical outcomes, such as in breast cancer. However, its precise role in breast cancer progression remains unclear. We integrated clinical breast cancer tissue speci...

title: Bioinformatics analysis of PFKFB family in pan-cancer and preliminary exploration of PFKFB4 in bladder cancer.
snippet: The phosphofructokinase-fructose bisphosphatases (PFKFBs) family is critical in tumors and the immune system.
